title: LCL
wikitext:
{{construction}}

'''LCL''' is an amber-colored, translucent liquid, which allows an Eva pilot to mentally link with their [[Evangelions|Evangelion Unit]].  The [[Entry Plug]] of an Eva Unit, containing its cockpit, is completely flooded with LCL, and, because it is oxygenated<ref>The LCL fluid inside of the Entry Plug is oxygenated, thus when Ramiel's energy beam strikes Eva Unit 01 in Episodes 5 and 6 and Shinji is being "boiled alive", you can see that the heat is causing all of the oxygen to bubble out of solution.</ref>, upon being submerged Eva pilots can "breathe" the liquid (similar to real-life experiments involving [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liquid_breathing#Fiction liquid breathing]).

Upon Evangelion activation, an electrical current is run through the LCL and it undergoes a phase shift, after which its density, opacity and viscosity appears to approach that of air. However, the LCL remains in a liquid phase and does not undergo a transition:  the pilots appear to be surrounded by air, however occasionally bubbles will float away from their mouths, revealing that they are still in a liquid medium.

It is stated several times in the series that LCL smells "like blood".  LCL is, in fact, the blood of the Second Angel, [[Lilith]], which is restrained in the deepest level of Nerv HQ, "[[Terminal Dogma]]".  The "LCL Plant" in Terminal Dogma is actually an entire lake formed from the blood of Lilith. 

LCL shares similar properties with the hypothetical "primordial ooze" from which life on Earth first evolved.  This is because non-Angel life on Earth actually originally evolved from the LCL spread by Lilith when she landed on Earth. During Third Impact, Lilith's [[Anti A.T. Field]] causes the [[A.T. Field|A.T. Fields]] of human beings and all other Lilith-based life to collapse, reverting their bodies to puddles of LCL.

LCL apparently has sources in addition to Lilith's body. In [[Episode 20]], [[Shinji Ikari|Shinji's]] body reforms from LCL within the [[core]] of [[Evangelion Unit-01|Eva-01]]. LCL is also seen escaping [[Evangelion Unit-00|Eva-00's]] core in [[Episode 23|Episode 23']] and Eva-01's (again) in [[Episode 26']]. 

An amber-colored fluid, visually identical to LCL, escapes from Leliel's body (the ultra-flat "shadow") when the bullets of Eva-01's handgun strike in [[Episode 16]].

==What does L.C.L. stand for?==
The term LCL is confirmed to be an acronym since it is consistently spelled as "L.C.L." on computer displays. The full form of "L.C.L." has never been revealed. 




*It is unknown what the initials "LCL" stand for, but we do know what they '''don't''' mean.  According to the [[Evangelion: Death and Rebirth]] theatrical program (special edition):  
::''"Incidentally, the widely circulated idea that L.C.L is the abbreviation of "Link Connected Liquid" is incorrect."''  
:However, nowhere does it say what it '''does mean.''' Why they would go out of their way to say what it ''doesn't'' stand for, but not go on to explain what it does stand for, is unknown, and a cause of much fan frustration.  [[Evangelion Chronicle]] suggests that one of the Ls stands for "Lilith". In the [[Manga]] the Dossier at the beginning of a chapter states that LCL '''does mean''' "Link Connected Liquid".

[[Image:LCL.JPG|thumb|Dossier of LCL in the Manga saying LCL stands for Link Connect Liquid]]


==In Other Media==
{{Rebuildcontentstart}}
*In the [[Rebuild of Evangelion]] continuity, LCL is colored blood red, not orange.
**Here, LCL is used in a dilute form to ease the strain on the pilot. This is seen in [[Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance]], when Gendo increases the concentration of LCL in Shinjis cockpit to the limit in order to avoid further damage to the Geofront from his outburst after forcing Eva-01 to destroy Eva-03 using the [[Dummy System]]. The concentration increase resulted in Shinji losing conciousness, which made it possible to detain him.
{{Rebuildcontentend}}

==Notes==
*Due to its orange color, LCL is often referred to as [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tang_%28drink%29 Tang™], an orange juice drink, by fans. Instead of saying "reverting to LCL," fans will often say, "(character) was Tanged", "Tangification", etc.  
**The color of LCL was changed to blood red instead of orange for ''Rebuild of Evangelion'', thus depriving fans of further comparisons of it to Tang.

*The Spanish word for "sky" or "heaven" is "El Cielo". The Sea of LCL in End of Evangelion seems to be in space. ''If'' this similarity is deliberate and not a coincidence, it makes LCL a wordplay on "heaven".
*Looking at the similarities and references of LCL to the hypothetical primordial soup of life, a possible meaning to the acronym LCL could very well be "Life Component Liquid", or a derivative thereof.
*In the Old Testament/Torah blood is equated with the lowest aspect of the soul in qabalah called nepesh (נפש).  See Leviticus (ויקרא) xvii:xi and Deuteronomy (דברים) xii:xxiii.

==References==
{{reflist}}

[[Category:A to Z]] [[Category:Terminology]]
html:
"We've seen it. The proof of its construction was very useful."
This article or section is under construction, and needs to be further expanded.
Please provide input on the talk page, or help build this page.

LCL is an amber-colored, translucent liquid, which allows an Eva pilot to mentally link with their Evangelion Unit. The Entry Plug of an Eva Unit, containing its cockpit, is completely flooded with LCL, and, because it is oxygenated[1], upon being submerged Eva pilots can "breathe" the liquid (similar to real-life experiments involving liquid breathing).

Upon Evangelion activation, an electrical current is run through the LCL and it undergoes a phase shift, after which its density, opacity and viscosity appears to approach that of air. However, the LCL remains in a liquid phase and does not undergo a transition: the pilots appear to be surrounded by air, however occasionally bubbles will float away from their mouths, revealing that they are still in a liquid medium.

It is stated several times in the series that LCL smells "like blood". LCL is, in fact, the blood of the Second Angel, Lilith, which is restrained in the deepest level of Nerv HQ, "Terminal Dogma". The "LCL Plant" in Terminal Dogma is actually an entire lake formed from the blood of Lilith.

LCL shares similar properties with the hypothetical "primordial ooze" from which life on Earth first evolved. This is because non-Angel life on Earth actually originally evolved from the LCL spread by Lilith when she landed on Earth. During Third Impact, Lilith's Anti A.T. Field causes the A.T. Fields of human beings and all other Lilith-based life to collapse, reverting their bodies to puddles of LCL.

LCL apparently has sources in addition to Lilith's body. In Episode 20, Shinji's body reforms from LCL within the core of Eva-01. LCL is also seen escaping Eva-00's core in Episode 23' and Eva-01's (again) in Episode 26'.

An amber-colored fluid, visually identical to LCL, escapes from Leliel's body (the ultra-flat "shadow") when the bullets of Eva-01's handgun strike in Episode 16.

Contents

  • 1 What does L.C.L. stand for?
  • 2 In Other Media
  • 3 Notes
  • 4 References

What does L.C.L. stand for?[edit]

The term LCL is confirmed to be an acronym since it is consistently spelled as "L.C.L." on computer displays. The full form of "L.C.L." has never been revealed.



  • It is unknown what the initials "LCL" stand for, but we do know what they don't mean. According to the Evangelion: Death and Rebirth theatrical program (special edition):
"Incidentally, the widely circulated idea that L.C.L is the abbreviation of "Link Connected Liquid" is incorrect."
However, nowhere does it say what it does mean. Why they would go out of their way to say what it doesn't stand for, but not go on to explain what it does stand for, is unknown, and a cause of much fan frustration. Evangelion Chronicle suggests that one of the Ls stands for "Lilith". In the Manga the Dossier at the beginning of a chapter states that LCL does mean "Link Connected Liquid".


In Other Media[edit]

  • In the Rebuild of Evangelion continuity, LCL is colored blood red, not orange.
    • Here, LCL is used in a dilute form to ease the strain on the pilot. This is seen in Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance, when Gendo increases the concentration of LCL in Shinjis cockpit to the limit in order to avoid further damage to the Geofront from his outburst after forcing Eva-01 to destroy Eva-03 using the Dummy System. The concentration increase resulted in Shinji losing conciousness, which made it possible to detain him.

Notes[edit]

  • Due to its orange color, LCL is often referred to as Tang™, an orange juice drink, by fans. Instead of saying "reverting to LCL," fans will often say, "(character) was Tanged", "Tangification", etc.
    • The color of LCL was changed to blood red instead of orange for Rebuild of Evangelion, thus depriving fans of further comparisons of it to Tang.
  • The Spanish word for "sky" or "heaven" is "El Cielo". The Sea of LCL in End of Evangelion seems to be in space. If this similarity is deliberate and not a coincidence, it makes LCL a wordplay on "heaven".
  • Looking at the similarities and references of LCL to the hypothetical primordial soup of life, a possible meaning to the acronym LCL could very well be "Life Component Liquid", or a derivative thereof.
  • In the Old Testament/Torah blood is equated with the lowest aspect of the soul in qabalah called nepesh (נפש). See Leviticus (ויקרא) xvii:xi and Deuteronomy (דברים) xii:xxiii.

References[edit]

  1. ↑ The LCL fluid inside of the Entry Plug is oxygenated, thus when Ramiel's energy beam strikes Eva Unit 01 in Episodes 5 and 6 and Shinji is being "boiled alive", you can see that the heat is causing all of the oxygen to bubble out of solution.

further comparisons of it to Tang.

The Spanish word for "sky" or "heaven" is "El Cielo". The Sea of LCL in End of Evangelion seems to be in space. If this similarity is deliberate and not a coincidence, it makes LCL a wordplay on "heaven".
Looking at the similarities and references of LCL to the hypothetical primordial soup of life, a possible meaning to the acronym LCL could very well be "Life Component Liquid", or a derivative thereof.
In the Old Testament/Torah blood is equated with the lowest aspect of the soul in qabalah called nepesh (נפש).  See Leviticus (ויקרא) xvii:xi and Deuteronomy (דברים) xii:xxiii.
